# Echo Persistence — Chapter II · The local store

> CubDB in depth — the append-only immutable B-tree under the native engine, and what those two words make possible. Crash-safe commits, free snapshots, MVCC and time travel, and recovery/replay/feed as one fold. Three modules, each a sub-folder with a hub `index.md` plus three dives. Links are to the readable `.md` sources (each has a rendered `.html` sibling).

## Navigation

- **Up: course root** — [../llms.txt](../llms.txt) · the course landing/map — [../index.md](../index.md)
- **Prev:** Chapter I · Foundations — [../foundations/llms.txt](../foundations/llms.txt) · **Next:** Chapter III · The engines — [../engines/llms.txt](../engines/llms.txt)

## Module 4 · CubDB architecture

> The durable local tier is a single embedded database: CubDB, an append-only, immutable B-tree on one file. Every property the native engine relies on — crash-safe commits, free snapshots, the page log itself — is a consequence of append-only + immutable.

- **Module hub** — [cubdb/index.md](cubdb/index.md)
- **Dive 4.1 · The append-only file** — commits append; the last header is the truth; crash mid-commit and the torn tail is discarded — [cubdb/the-append-only-file.md](cubdb/the-append-only-file.md)
- **Dive 4.2 · The immutable B-tree** — copy-on-write; update a key and only the root-to-leaf path is rewritten, the old tree left whole — [cubdb/the-immutable-btree.md](cubdb/the-immutable-btree.md)
- **Dive 4.3 · Compaction** — the file only grows; dead nodes pile up; compact copies the live set to a fresh file and reclaims the rest — [cubdb/compaction.md](cubdb/compaction.md)

## Module 5 · MVCC & time travel

> Multiversion concurrency control is what you get for free from "old roots stay valid." A snapshot is a retained root, so readers never lock and never block the writer, and "the state at any past LSN" is just the root that was current then.

- **Module hub** — [mvcc-time-travel/index.md](mvcc-time-travel/index.md)
- **Dive 5.1 · Coexisting versions** — many roots, one node pool; click a version and watch which nodes it reaches, mostly shared, a few its own — [mvcc-time-travel/coexisting-versions.md](mvcc-time-travel/coexisting-versions.md)
- **Dive 5.2 · Lock-free readers** — start a reader, let the writer commit, and watch the reader keep reading its pinned version while the head races ahead — [mvcc-time-travel/lock-free-readers.md](mvcc-time-travel/lock-free-readers.md)
- **Dive 5.3 · Checkout & GC** — open the Volume at any past LSN with a scrubber, then run GC and watch old roots reclaimed except the one a reader pins — [mvcc-time-travel/checkout-and-gc.md](mvcc-time-travel/checkout-and-gc.md)

## Module 6 · Replay & recovery

> Booting a fresh process, catching up a replica, and feeding a subscriber changes are the same operation: replay the immutable log from some LSN forward to the head. Only the starting LSN and the source of the bytes differ.

- **Module hub** — [replay-and-recovery/index.md](replay-and-recovery/index.md)
- **Dive 6.1 · One log, three replays** — three cursors on one log; advance each to the head and watch the apply step be identical — [replay-and-recovery/one-log-three-replays.md](replay-and-recovery/one-log-three-replays.md)
- **Dive 6.2 · Green boot** — blue serves while green seeds from the snapshot, replays the tail, and traffic cuts over with zero downtime — [replay-and-recovery/green-boot.md](replay-and-recovery/green-boot.md)
- **Dive 6.3 · Replica catch-up & the feed** — a replica pulls segments after its LSN until lag is zero, and the change feed is that same ordered stream — [replay-and-recovery/replica-catch-up-and-the-feed.md](replay-and-recovery/replica-catch-up-and-the-feed.md)
